---
title: "Gen2: Systematic Feature Engineering"
subtitle: "AA DFW Hub | 804,728 flights | 12 versions x 9 experiments = 108 experiments"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-location: right
    toc-depth: 2
    number-sections: false
    page-layout: full
    smooth-scroll: true
---

```{=html}
<nav style="margin-bottom:1.5rem;">
  <small><a href="index.html">Home</a> › <a href="research-overview-en.html">Research Overview</a> › Gen2</small>
  <br>
  <a href="research-overview-en.html" style="display:inline-block;margin-top:0.5rem;padding:0.3rem 0.8rem;border:1px solid #ccc;border-radius:4px;text-decoration:none;font-size:0.9rem;">← Back to Research Overview</a>
</nav>
```

::: {.panel-tabset}

## Overview

**Dataset:** AA DFW Hub, 804,728 flights, delay rate 23.3% (arrival >=15min)

**Temporal split:**

| Set | Period |
|---|---|
| Train | 2021-2022 |
| Validation | 2023 H1 |
| Test | 2023 H2 |

**Experiment matrix:** 3 feature sets (Clean 11, T-2h 45, T-2h+Enhanced 44) x 3 models (RF, XGBoost, XGB+CatBoost) = 9 experiments per version

**Core principle:** Zero data leakage — all features must be available before T-2h

::: {.callout-tip}
## Gen2 Core Lesson
Only genuinely new external information can improve AUC; same-source re-encoding is entirely ineffective.
:::

## V1 Baseline {#v1}

**Content:** Schedule features + DFW METAR weather + simple tail chain (previous flight delay)

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
**AUC 0.7167** — far below the target of 0.80
:::

**Problem:** Insufficient feature information; richer feature sources needed.

**Next step:** V2 adds literature-driven features.

## V2 Literature Features {#v2}

**New features:**

- **Multi-hop tail chain:** Delay from 2nd and 3rd previous flights, cumulative delay
- **Airport congestion:** Hourly departure count, 3h average delay, 3h delay rate
- **Weather severity composite index**

::: {.callout-tip}
## Largest single improvement across all 12 versions
AUC **+0.0185**
:::

This is the only case where "same-source but literature-validated feature engineering" worked. Multi-hop tail chain captured the delay propagation effect.

**Next step:** Continue exploring new data sources.

## V3-V5 Internal Recombination {#v3v5}

### V3: Two-stage prediction

- **Concept:** Stage I XGBoost predicts departure delay (continuous value), Stage II uses the prediction as a feature

::: {.callout-warning}
## Failed
Stage I R-squared was only **0.18**, severely limiting Stage II quality. Same-source meta-features do not work — first confirmation of this pattern.
:::

### V4: Destination METAR weather (11 features)

- Added destination NOAA IEM METAR (observations at T-2h before arrival)

::: {.callout-warning}
## Failed
Only **+0.0016**. Destination weather snapshot is an information bottleneck — a single time-point weather observation 2 hours before arrival cannot capture in-flight weather changes.
:::

### V5: BTS monthly airport statistics (6 features)

- Added monthly airport statistics: delay rate, avg delay, delay std dev (origin/dest, 3 each)

::: {.callout-warning}
## Failed
Negative result. Monthly granularity is too coarse for individual flight prediction; categorical airport codes already implicitly contain this information.
:::

## V6 SHAP + Optuna {#v6}

**Two components:**

1. **SHAP backward elimination:** 49 → 35 features, +0.0009 on val
2. **Optuna 100-trial Bayesian HPO:** Found lr=0.006, depth=10, gamma=4.79

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
**Combined improvement +0.0047**
:::

Diminishing marginal returns from optimization. Feature space and model are approaching their ceiling; further HPO yields minimal returns.

## V7-V8 Redundant Features {#v7v8}

### V7: Temporal + Route features (8 new features)

- Added: `dest_delay_rate_7d/30d`, `route_daily_freq`, `aircraft_legs_today`, `dfw_delay_rate_1h/6h`

::: {.callout-warning}
## Worst across all 12 versions
**-0.0054**. Tree models with depth=10 already capture these temporal patterns automatically; manual features are entirely redundant.
:::

### V8: SHAP interaction features + Focal Loss

- 15 SHAP-identified interaction features (e.g., `dep_hour x wx_severity`)
- Also tested Focal Loss for class imbalance

::: {.callout-warning}
## Failed
Both negative. depth=10 already implicitly captures interaction effects; Focal Loss at 23.3% imbalance sacrifices AUC for F1.
:::

## V9 TAF Forecasts {#v9}

**Added 9 TAF features (IEM source):** forecast wind, gust, visibility, ceiling, precip (DFW + dest)

::: {.callout-tip}
## First positive result since V2
**+0.002**
:::

**Key distinction:** TAF is a weather "forecast," not an "observation" — fundamentally different from METAR.

**Why it works:** Brings in genuinely new external information — the meteorological forecast system's future predictions, not re-encoding of historical observations.

**Pattern confirmed:** "Only genuinely new external information can break through the AUC ceiling."

## V10-V12 All Negative {#v10v12}

### V10: Target Encoding + Network hub weather proxy

- Target Encoding for high-cardinality features (carrier, airport)
- Network Weather: Average weather across 9 major hubs as a system proxy

::: {.callout-warning}
## Failed
Both negative. TE is redundant for depth=10 XGBoost; hub weather is already covered by DFW local features.
:::

### V11: NOTEARS DAG + 3-model OOF stacking

- NOTEARS linear DAG for causal feature selection
- XGB + CatBoost + LightGBM three-model OOF stacking

::: {.callout-warning}
## Failed
Both negative. Linear NOTEARS is unsuitable for binary classification; stacking did not outperform single XGBoost.
:::

### V12: Delay-Absorption meta-feature

- Replicated Li (2025) AbsorbScore: Stage I CatBoost predicts delay absorption
- Stage I OOF AUC was only 0.6946

::: {.callout-warning}
## Failed
Negative result. Li (2025)'s AUC=0.898 relied on departure delay leakage; our strict version could not replicate it.
:::

## V9c Finalized {#v9c}

**Issue discovered:** V9 tail chain used scheduled turnaround threshold to determine whether the previous flight had arrived, potentially introducing subtle leakage.

**Fix:** Switched to actual arrival time for determination.

**Impact scope:**

- T-2h version: Only **1.04%** of flights affected
- T-24h version: **77.9%** affected, permanently abandoned

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
**AUC 0.7418** (-0.0022 vs V9) — This is the Gen2 final result
:::

V9c becomes the baseline for all subsequent analyses.

## Phase 1-4 Comprehensive Analysis {#phases}

### Phase 1: Literature Leakage Audit

Systematic review of data leakage in 12 papers:

- Only **2/12** used temporal split
- Only **2/12** had completely clean features
- **5/12** had severe or fatal leakage

::: {.callout-warning}
Papers with leakage reported AUC **0.87-0.99**; clean temporal studies reported **0.62-0.74**.
:::

Conclusion: Our AUC 0.7418 is competitive under methodologically rigorous conditions.

### Phase 2: Literature Method Reproduction

Reproduced 6 literature methods under the same temporal split conditions:

| Method | AUC Change |
|---|---|
| SMOTE | -0.028 |
| ADASYN | -0.029 |
| SelectKBest | -0.023 |
| FSFS | -0.018 |
| ATT-BI-LSTM | -0.028 |
| LightGBM | +0.008 |

Aeolus benchmark: All 4 GBDT models outperformed all 7 DL models.

### Phase 3: SHAP Feature Importance

::: {.callout-tip}
## Our contributions account for 42.8% of total SHAP

- **Tail Chain:** 22.7%
- **TAF Forecasts:** 12.4%
- **Dest METAR:** 7.7%
:::

### Phase 4: Ablation Study

**Incremental addition:**

| Feature Group | AUC | Delta |
|---|---|---|
| Schedule only | 0.6226 | — |
| + Aircraft/Infra | 0.6710 | +0.048 |
| + DFW METAR | 0.6928 | +0.022 |
| + Dest METAR | 0.7048 | +0.012 |
| + TAF | 0.6969 | -0.008 |
| + Tail Chain | 0.7279 | +0.031 |

**Leave-one-out:** Removing Tail Chain **-0.031** (most critical); removing Aircraft/Infra **-0.023**.

:::

```{=html}
<script>
window.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() {
  var hash = window.location.hash.replace('#', '');
  if (!hash) return;
  var tabs = document.querySelectorAll('[data-bs-toggle="tab"], [data-bs-toggle="pill"]');
  for (var i = 0; i < tabs.length; i++) {
    var target = tabs[i].getAttribute('data-bs-target') || tabs[i].getAttribute('href') || '';
    if (target.indexOf(hash) !== -1) {
      tabs[i].click();
      break;
    }
  }
});
</script>
```
